article_title: European Commission Confirms Data Breach After ShinyHunters Publish Stolen Europa.eu Records

content_preview.title: European Commission Confirms Data Breach After ShinyHunters Publish Stolen Europa.eu Records
content_preview.summary: The European Commission confirms a breach of its AWS-hosted Europa.eu platform after ShinyHunters published over 90 GB of stolen data. CERT-EU traces the intrusion to a supply chain attack on the Trivy security scanner.
content_preview.body_excerpt: The European Commission confirmed on March 30, 2026, that attackers had breached at least one Amazon Web Services account underpinning its Europa.eu web platform and stolen data belonging to the bloc's executive arm. The acknowledgment came days after the [ShinyHunters](https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/news/security/european-commission-confirms-data-breach-after-europaeu-hack/) cyber-extortion group published samples of what it claims is more than 350 gigabytes of exfiltrated material on its dar...

editor_notes.content_quality: Strong cybersecurity article with detailed technical timeline and clear attribution chain (TeamPCP initial access, ShinyHunters leak).
editor_notes.source_verification: 3 sources checked. BleepingComputer: VERIFIED — confirms March 30 confirmation, AWS breach, ShinyHunters, 350GB claimed, second incident after Feb MDM compromise. TechCrunch: VERIFIED — confirms CERT-EU dual attribution (TeamPCP + ShinyHunters). Infosecurity Magazine: VERIFIED — confirms 90GB published, email files, DKIM keys, NextCloud, Athena data, Nick Tausek quote, AWS STS technique. Note: Trivy supply chain detail attributed to Infosecurity but their extract focused on vishing/social engineering. The Trivy detail may come from a more complete version of the article not captured in summary.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Core claims verified: March 30 confirmation, AWS breach, ShinyHunters/TeamPCP dual attribution, data scope, Regnier and Tausek quotes, second incident timeline.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and balanced. Includes Commission's downplaying alongside security expert critique.
editor_notes.originality: No prior ShinyHunters or EU Commission breach coverage. New topic.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Solid cybersecurity article. All sources verified. Approved.
